title: Unauthenticated RPC admin endpoints allow runtime persistent-peer modification
vuln_class: Broken Access Control / Unauthenticated Administrative API
source_to_sink: HTTP request -> /persistent-peers (POST/DELETE) -> JSON body addr parsed -> NetworkRequest::add_persistent_peer/remove_persistent_peer invoked with no authz check
prerequisites: attacker can reach bound RPC address
impact: Remote attacker can mutate node peer set, forcing outbound connections and altering networking behavior; can degrade availability/isolation and potentially induce connections to attacker-controlled infrastructure.
evidence_summary: Router applies only extract_version middleware and no authentication middleware.
Handlers immediately execute add/remove operations after parsing body.
Unit tests show successful POST /persistent-peers request without any auth headers/tokens.
reproduction: Run node with --rpc.addr on reachable interface; send POST /persistent-peers with JSON {"addr":"/ip4/<attacker>/tcp/<port>/p2p/<peerid>"}; observe 200 {"status":"ok"}.
not_false_positive: This is an explicit write-capability endpoint affecting runtime network configuration, exposed over HTTP, with no credential checks in the request path.
severity: Medium (can become High in exposed deployments)
uncertainty: Exposure is deployment-dependent because RPC is optional and bind address is operator-selected.
